attacking_style: Does not typically initiate attacks; instead positions intelligently in early splits (crosswinds, strategic breaks) then uses superior fitness to emerge from chase groups in final kilometers.
team_tactics: INEOS deploys controlled, tempo-based finale strategy with van Baarle positioned to capitalize after team control work; benefits from crosswind splitting tactics ingrained from Sky/Ineos schooling.
terrain_preference: Cobbled classics and exposed crosswind sections where team control and positioning trump pure explosive power; shows surprising climbing range for sustained efforts on summit finishes.
vulnerability: Not a pure sprinter—relies on attrition and selection before final; team confusion or compromised sprint trains leave him without clear role (Tour de Pologne example).
key_patterns: Emerges from chase groups rather than initiating moves, using endurance advantage (Paris-Roubaix, Flanders)
Executes INEOS crosswind tactics with tactical savvy from Sky/Ineos background (Vuelta, Paris-Roubaix)
Shows climbing range beyond typical classics profile, effective on sustained mountain efforts (Paris-Nice)
Benefits from team-controlled finales where positioning trumps explosive power (Paris-Roubaix victory)
form_trajectory: positive: dramatic progression from 2021 time-cut elimination at Paris-Roubaix to 2022 Monument victory; elevated from domestique to race-winning leadership roles across 2021-2022
built_from: 2021 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
